Customer Complaint:- Check for Abnormal noise coming from engine compartment after 80Kmph._x000d_
vehicle was reported to our workshop with concerns about abnormal noise coming from the engine compartment after 80 Kmph._x000d_
We have driven the vehicle and found the concern to be genuine._x000d_
We have opened the Bonnet for any loose components and found black smoke(carbon deposit) on the bonnet at the Turbocharger spot._x000d_
We have dismantled the heat shield on the turbocharger and found a huge amount of carbon deposit inside._x000d_
We have inspected the Turbocharger clamp and found it in good condition._x000d_
We have loosened the clamp and found the gasket in an offset state than in the intended position._x000d_
We have tried correcting the position with the old gasket and clamp but still, the issue was not resolved._x000d_
We have recommended a Turbocharger gasket and clamp replacement._x000d_
Upon replacing the Turbocharger gasket and the V-Clamp the issue was resolved._x000d_
REF SCR NO:-S-1000000802516_x000d_

vehicle after delivery broke down on the way after a stop and never cranked and was towed back to work shop for investigation found vehicle was not cranking with no critical DTC recorded related to starting.Upon connecting the i-Smart (Scorpio N, as MIDA was not functional) to the vehicle, no critical error codes related to the starting system were detected. Preliminary checks of fuses and relays revealed no issues. Further investigation based on the wiring manual confirmed fuses 80A, F1-50A, F4-40A, F60-5A, F9-50A, F48-20A, and F38-5A were intact. However, continuity testing identified a disconnect between C405F-14 and C532K-80. Examination of the engine room wiring harness showed damage near the firewall, likely caused by a sharp object. After repairing the harness, the vehicle started normally. The customer, having just taken delivery, requested a complete replacement of the damaged harness, which was completed to ensure satisfaction._x000d_

GLASS DOOR BACK HIGH GAS STRUT NOT FUNCTIONING NORMALLY _x000d_
     When the rear glass door is opened, it does not stay in the raised position and slowly descends under its own weight. This creates a safety hazard and suggests that the gas strut is no longer providing sufficient support or damping. It is likely that the gas strut has lost internal pressure or experienced a seal failure, resulting in reduced gas pressure. This prevents the strut from effectively counter balancing the weight of the glass door, causing it to lower gradually or unexpectedly. Note: Compared with another vehicle for same the concern vehicle gas strut is not functioning normally. Hence we replace the part GAS STRUT LIFT GLASS and found issue got solved. For your reference details enclosed in SCR No. S-1000000901693

glass door back gas strut not working.The customer reported that the back glass door was not opening and closing properly. Upon inspection, we found that the RH gas strut was malfunctioning. Physically, the RH gas strut was unable to lift the glass door, causing the RH side to drop automatically. To resolve the issue, we replaced the RH gas strut, which restored the door's proper function REF SCR S-1000000828734

PELASE EFER TAR - T-KOF817020. The subject vehicle was reported to us for the concern of infotainment screen going blank sometimes, but the cluster is working normally. When analyzing the symptom, we observed that the issue is with loading the tiles only, and the status bar and display backlighting are still visible. This issue persists until the next ignition toggle and repeats randomly after a deep sleep. As per the diagnostic manual, we have checked the resistance values of the A2B wiring from the MIC to the silver box and from the mic to the AMP. We found no short to battery or short to ground conditions. We also removed and reconnected all electrical connectors on the silver box, infotainment screen, and mic assembly. Vendor has provided new infotainment and the old was given back to them. After part replacement we have done silverbox flashing and found good condition.

REFER SCR S-1000000802124. The vehicle was reported to our dealership on 28-12-2024 for the complaint of poor braking efficiency and pedal sinking issues. As per the customer's concern, our team tested the vehicle and validated it. After parking the vehicle for a few minutes our team test-drove the vehicle and noticed that the pedal sank while applying the brake pedal after a few minutes of usage it worked fine. During the preliminary inspection, we noticed that the brake fluid level in the reservoir was okay. REFER SCR S-1000000854341. Vehicle was reported in our dealership for the complaint of Engine Coolant Leakage from Dosing Module Cooling Circuit. Clip & Connector Inspection: No issues found. So we replaced HOSE ASSY DEF DOSAGE OUTLET and concern resolved

REFER THE SCR: S-1000000872784, THE VEHICLE WAS REPORTED TO THE WORKSHOP AS A BREAKDOWN. ACCORDING TO THE CUSTOMER VERBATIM, THERE WAS A SUDDEN SOUND, AND THEN THE VEHICLE SHUT DOWN. REFER SCR S-1000000907054. The vehicle was reported with an intermittent OBD lamp concern. During diagnosing the EMS ECU, DTC P06DA00 ¿ Variable Oil Pump Open Load was found in a healed condition. As per the diagnostic procedure, we checked fuse F25 (10A) for any blown or loose contact and found no issues. Voltage at pin 1 of connector C351 was measured at 12.37V with ignition ON. Signal wire continuity from VDOP (pin 2) to EMS controller showed 0.3 ohms resistance, and the VDOP valve resistance measured is 9.9 ohms, which is within the specification of 10.5 ¿ 0.5 ohms. Since no abnormalities were found, but the issue was intermittent, so we replaced the VDOP control valve for an observation and conducted an extensive test drive of approximately 40 km, during which the concern did not reoccur. Customer had complaint about vehicle Sunroof not open and close by pressing sunroof switch - The vehicle was reported with a sunroof that wouldn't open or close when the sunroof switch was pressed. After inspection, it was confirmed that the customer's complaint was accurate. Upon connecting the I-smart tool, DTC B114E-09 (Sunroof/sunshade fault) was found. The fuses and relay were checked and found to be in good condition. The sunroof-related wiring harness was inspected for cuts or damage, but none were found. The connectors for the sunroof motor, sunroof switch, and MBFM were all checked, and no loose contacts were found.To resolve the issue, the sunroof motor assembly was replaced, and the sunroof learning process was performed. After the work was completed, the sunroof operated correctly, opening and closing as expected. The issue was resolved, and the sunroof is now functioning properly.VIDE S-1000000826629

The customer reported that the sunroof was not opening or closing. Using the I-Smart diagnostic tool, no related DTCs were found. All sunroof-related fuses and relays were inspected and found to be in good condition. Power supply was checked at the sunroof motor connector during switch operation, but no voltage was present. However, power was found at the sunroof switch connector, indicating the switch might be faulty. The sunroof switch was replaced, and after operating the new switch, power supply at the sunroof motor connector was measured at 12.32V. Despite this, the sunroof motor did not operate, leading to the conclusion that the motor was faulty. The sunroof motor was replaced, after which the motor functioned correctly. Sunroof learning was performed, and the issue was successfully resolved.VIDE SCR NO S-1000000863467

The vehicle came in with a concern that the FPAS sensor was not working properly. Upon inspection, we confirmed the issue and found that the sensor was faulty. To resolve the concern, we replaced the defective FPAS (FRONT PARKING SENSOR) sensor. After the replacement, the sensor functioned normally, and the issue was resolved."

During inspection of the customer complaint regarding reverse parking error, we thoroughly checked the vehicle during reverse parking operation. It was observed that there was no beep sound or visual indication on the instrument cluster. Upon detailed diagnosis, we found that the sensor parking control unit (non-ECU) was faulty, which caused the reverse parking sensors to malfunction and not communicate with the cluster. This failure prevented any warning signals or guidance from being displayed during reverse maneuvering. The root cause was identified as an internal fault within the non-ECU sensor module. To rectify the issue, the faulty sensor module was replaced under warranty. Post-replacement, the system functioned normally, and the customer's concern was fully resolved.

The right headlamp functioned properly, while the left low/high beam was non-functional. Fuses, relays, and wiring harnesses were checked and found OK. Vehicle THAR ROXX AX7L was reported to our workshop with the complaint that the left-side headlamp DRL was not glowing properly, appearing slightly dim. The vehicle was reported to the workshop with a complaint that FM/AM radio was not working. Upon inspection, the complaint was confirmed. Using the i-Smart diagnostic tool, DTCs B1007-13 (Radio Antenna Open Circuit) and B1007-11 (Radio Antenna Short to Ground) were found. As per the diagnostic manual, stepwise inspection was carried out. The antenna connections were checked and found properly assembled with no loose contacts. The antenna connector was removed and reassembled, but the issue persisted. As the final step, the radio antenna was replaced with a new Antenna Rod Assembly (Part No. 1502AAA01071N). After replacement, the FM/AM functionality was restored. Hence, the antenna rod was confirmed to be faulty, and the issue was resolved.

A vehicle was reported to the workshop with a complaint of abnormal noise during 3rd gear shifting. Upon inspection, all relevant parameters were checked, and the gearbox was dismantled for detailed investigation. The gear cable was found to be in good condition. However, the inspection revealed that certain internal components were faulty. Specifically, the bush, 5th drive gear, and the 3rd/4th synchro assembly were found to be defective. These faulty parts were identified as the root cause of the abnormal noise occurring during 3rd gear shifting. Replacement of the affected components is recommended to resolve the issue and restore proper gear function. Vide scr no s-1000000865976

Vehicle not crank.The Breakdown Vehicle was brought to our workshop with a complaint about starting issues. The engine cranks, but STARTER SLEEPAGE. Then, we connected the I-smart and found no DTC related to the starting problem. We checked the following points: Battery voltage was measured at 12.60 V.A 12-volt supply to the starter motor was confirmed to be 12V. The starter relay control and power circuits are functioning properly, with no burnt relay. The starter motor itself is faulty, as it did not draw current; only the supply voltage at the starter motor was 12 volts for 8 seconds before being cut off by the EMS ECU. We checked the clutch for sticking, but it was okay; the clutch system is working properly. The starter circuit components are ok, with no short circuits found.We swapped the STARTER motor in the vehicle, and it started normally.We replaced the starter motor assembly, and the issue was resolved. scr no.S-1000000895675_x000d_

REFER TAR NO: T-WBP837692 Vehicle reported with start and stop switch not responding full time. The MIDA diagnostic tool is connected but is not communicating with ECU's because the ignition is not turned on. Battery volt found 12.5v.  Then we checked PKE ECU supply pin 9, 10 in the c265 connector it found 12.3 v. Then checked the ground supply wire continuity to the body ground at pins 1 and 2 of connector C265 and found continuity is available 0.3 ohm. Then we checked CAN circuit resistance between pins 5 and 13 of connector C265 and got 60.2 ohms resistance. So as per approval replaced PKE ECU then flashing done and concern resolved.

Check engine lamp issue and as per lamp status. observed DTC in EMS ECU P06DA00 Oil Pump Open Load [caf¿] Further with the help of tek-digital we carried out diagnosis for the above mentioned. Step 1 : CHECK OPEN CIRCUIT OF VARIABLE OIL PUMP SUPPLY. Inspected 10A Fuse F25 in EC found in good condition. Measured the voltage between pin 1 of C351 and ground, value found to be 12.38V.Acceptance criteria: Battery Voltage. Step 2 : CHECK OPEN CIRCUIT OF VARIABLE OIL PUMP SIGNAL WIRE. Measured the resistance between pin 2 of C 351 and pin 21 of C 532 K, value found to be infinity ohms. Acceptance criteria: Less than 1 ohms. Step 3 : We checked resistance between pin 21 of C 532 K and pin 22 of C 400 F, value found to be less than 1 ohms. Next checked resistance between pin pin 22 of C 400 M and pin 2 of C 351, value found to be infinity ohms. We fitted the new oil pump externally and error code has changed from current to testing bending, We remove the affected oil

The vehicle was reported to our workshop with a complaint that the IS screen remains on even after switching off the ignition. We connected MIDA and found the DTC B132C 12 (Accessory Relay Feedback ¿ Short Circuit to Supply Error) active in the PKE module. Since the DTC could not be cleared, we initially suspected a faulty PKE. To confirm, we swapped the PKE with another vehicle, but the concern remained. We then connected the original PKE to a different vehicle and found that the issue did not occur there, indicating that the PKE itself was not faulty. Upon further inspection, we observed approximately 5 volts at the F46 output terminal with the ignition off, where it should read 0 volts. This was verified by comparing with a known-good vehicle. Based on these findings, the issue was traced to a fault in the IP wiring harness. To resolve the concern, we replaced the defective wiring. After replacement concern is resolved  For more details, please refer to TAR No.: T-GGV796124.

A concern vehicle was reported regarding the permanent screen blank of the infotainment system and instrument cluster. Upon inspection, we found that the F34 10A IP fuse was blown. However, after refitting the okay fuse the screen still did not wake up._x000d_

We replaced the fuses, and screen wake up. but a new concern is revealed in this vehicle Even after ignition off, the infotainment system remained active and did not turn off. Further investigation revealed that even after disconnecting the C34 fuse, 12V was present on pin 1 of C33a, which is abnormal. To rectify the concern, we replaced the IP wiring. The said vehicle is our stock vehicle with the concern of vehicle IGN not getting ON. And no ECU's are communicating with the MIDA tool. We have checked & found the concern to be true. The vehicle IGN is not getting ON & vehicle doesn't start. Then we have connected the MIDA & tried to check for the DTC's in it. But VIN NO. & MODEL NO. are not getting validated. The vehicle is not communicating with the diagnostics tool in anyway. Then we checked the PKE ECU wiring. The diagnosis is as follows:-We checked the pre-ignition supply on pin number 9 & pin number 10 of connector C265 & found 12.13V.We have checked the continuity from pin number 17 & pin number 18 of connector C265 to pin number 80 of connector C 532K & found the continuity to be OK We have checked the pre-ignition supply on pin number 1 of connector C72_L (IGN RELAY) & found 12.12V.We have checked the continuity from pin number 5 of C72_L (IGN RELAY). SO WE REP THE PKE ECU.TAR NO T-NYG836891

Vehicle Only Moving in 4x4 Low Gear. Vehicle was reported with the concern that it only moves in 4x4 low gear. During inspection, we found that an abnormal "ghar ghar" noise was coming from the transfer case while shifting from 4H to 2H. The vehicle has only been driven for 13,899 kilometers. We checked and confirmed there was no oil leakage from the transfer case. On draining the transfer case oil, we found approximately 1.2 liters of oil. Upon inspection of the drained oil, we observed the presence of emery (metal particles) in it. To resolve the issue, we replaced the transfercase assembly under warranty. After replacement, a trial was carried out and the concern was found to be resolved. TAR Number :-T-EEU819455.
